codeReview.summary: The feature implements the requested recursive nested-sensitive seam correctly. The new SensitiveShape abstraction carries the parent field's resolved crypto context into Child, Option<Child>, and Vec<Child> leaves, and the focused runtime/store tests cover plaintext caller results plus encrypted-at-rest storage for the approved shapes.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The repo now has a concrete recursive sensitive runtime seam (SensitiveShape) that is central to nested secure behavior, but .factory/library/sensitive-crypto.md still describes the rule only at a high level and does not record the actual abstraction name or where it lives. That omission makes later workers rediscover the implementation seam by source inspection.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: core/src/lib.rs:103 defines SensitiveShape; macros/src/lib.rs:1263-1307 routes secure fields through it; .factory/library/sensitive-crypto.md mentions inherited parent-field context but not the SensitiveShape seam.

summary: Pass. Diff and transcript match the contract for nested Child, Option<Child>, and Vec<Child> secure shapes, with no blocking or non-blocking code issues found. One shared-state knowledge gap remains: the library docs do not yet capture the concrete SensitiveShape abstraction that now underpins nested secure recursion.
